theorem expected_involution_value_eq_one {α : Type*} [Fintype α]
  (f : α → ℝ) (I : Set α → ℝ) (hI : IsProbabilityMeasure I):
  expected_involution_value f I = 1 := by
  have h₁ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₂ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  have h₃ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₄ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  have h₅ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₆ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  have h₇ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₈ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  have h₉ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₁₀ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  have h₁₁ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
  have h₁₂ : ∑' p : α, I {p} = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
  -- Use the fact that the sum of the probabilities is 1 and the linearity of I
  -- to show that the sum of the involution terms is also 1.
  calc
    expected_involution_value f I = ∑' p : α, I {p} := rfl
    _ = 1 := by
      -- Use the fact that the sum of the probabilities is 1 to conclude the proof.
      have h_sum : ∑' p : α, I {p} = 1 := hI.2
      -- Use the sum to simplify the expression.
      rw [h_sum]
      <;>
      simp_all [tsum_eq_single]
      <;>
      aesop

        exact (inferInstance : Nonempty d₂)
      exact this
    -- Use the identity maps to show that Λ emulates itself
    refine' ⟨CPTPMap.id, CPTPMap.id, _⟩
    simp [CPTPMap.id]
    <;>
    (try { simp_all [CPTPMap.id] }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [CPTPMap.fst] }) <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try { norm_num }) <;>
    (try { simp_all [CPTPMap.piProd] })
  · -- Prove that logb 2 1 ≥ 0 * 1 and the identity map approximates itself with ε=ε
    have h₁ : Real.logb 2 (1 : ℕ) = 0 := by norm_num [Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq]
    simp_all [h₁]
    <;>
    (try { norm_num }) <;>

    (try { simp_all [CPTPMap.id] }) <;>
    (try { norm_num [CPTPMap.id] }) <;>
    (try { linarith }) <;>
    (try { aesop })

matrix) has trace `Fintype.card d₁ ≥ 1 > 0`, and its image must have trace `0`, which is a contradiction unless the linear map is not trace preserving. 

Thus, no `CPTPMap d₁ d₂` exists if `d₂` is empty and `d₁` is not.
